sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
Directions: In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, 
you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be 
repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the 
best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are the exams corrected yet?
(man)No, but they'll be corrected by noon.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'll correct the exams this afternoon.

reason.en: The man states the exams will be corrected BY noon, not that he will correct them THIS afternoon. This option changes the timeframe and the action.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan ujian akan dikoreksi SEBELUM tengah hari, bukan bahwa dia akan mengoreksinya SORE INI. Opsi ini mengubah kerangka waktu dan tindakan.

options.text: The exam will be at noon.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the correction of exams, not the time the exam will take place. The word 'noon' refers to the completion of the correction, not the exam itself.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang pengoreksian ujian, bukan waktu ujian akan dilaksanakan. Kata 'tengah hari' merujuk pada selesainya pengoreksian, bukan ujian itu sendiri.

options.text: He will collect the exams at 12:00.

reason.en: The dialogue mentions 'corrected,' not 'collect.' This is a common trap using similar-sounding words.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut menyebutkan 'dikoreksi' (corrected), bukan 'dikumpulkan' (collect). Ini adalah jebakan umum yang menggunakan kata-kata yang bunyinya mirip.

options.text: The tests will be graded by noon.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the correct answer paraphrases the man's statement. 'Corrected' is synonymous with 'graded,' and 'by noon' means the task will be completed at that time.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, jawaban yang benar memparafrasekan pernyataan pria itu. 'Corrected' (dikoreksi) sinonim dengan 'graded' (dinilai), dan 'by noon' (pada tengah hari) berarti tugas tersebut akan selesai pada waktu itu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Has Martha's visa arrived yet?
(man)I think it arrived last month.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Martha applied for a visa last month.

reason.en: The man states that the visa arrived last month, not that Martha applied for it last month. This option misinterprets the timing of the event.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa visa tiba bulan lalu, bukan bahwa Martha mengajukannya bulan lalu. Opsi ini salah menafsirkan waktu kejadian.

options.text: Martha's visa will last for only a month.

reason.en: The dialogue mentions the visa arrived last month, but it says nothing about its duration or how long it will last. This is an unsupported inference.
reason.id: Dialog menyebutkan visa tiba bulan lalu, tetapi tidak mengatakan apa pun tentang durasinya atau berapa lama masa berlakunya. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang tidak didukung.

options.text: Martha arrived last month without her visa.

reason.en: The dialogue implies the visa arrived, so it's unlikely Martha arrived without it. This option contradicts the information given.
reason.id: Dialog menyiratkan bahwa visa telah tiba, jadi kecil kemungkinannya Martha datang tanpanya. Opsi ini bertentangan dengan informasi yang diberikan.

options.text: One month ago Martha got her visa.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man says 'I think it arrived last month.' This means that Martha received her visa one month ago. The phrase 'arrived last month' is synonymous with 'One month ago Martha got her visa.'
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pria itu berkata 'Saya pikir itu tiba bulan lalu.' Ini berarti Martha mendapatkan visanya satu bulan yang lalu. Frasa 'tiba bulan lalu' identik dengan 'Satu bulan yang lalu Martha mendapatkan visanya.'

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)What did the professor do in the first lass? I missed it because I was late.
(woman) She outlined the course requirements.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor described what the students should do.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'She outlined the course requirements' is paraphrased by option [0] 'The professor described what the students should do.' 'Outlined' is synonymous with 'described,' and 'course requirements' is synonymous with 'what the students should do.'
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'She outlined the course requirements' diparafrasekan oleh opsi [0] 'The professor described what the students should do.' 'Outlined' sinonim dengan 'described,' dan 'course requirements' sinonim dengan 'what the students should do.'

options.text: There was a long line to register for the required class.

reason.en: Option [1] is incorrect because the dialogue does not mention anything about a long line or registration. This is a distractor that introduces irrelevant information.
reason.id: Opsi [1] salah karena dialog tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang antrean panjang atau pendaftaran. Ini adalah pengalih perhatian yang memperkenalkan informasi yang tidak relevan.

options.text: It is a requirement for each professor to teach at least one course.

reason.en: Option [2] is incorrect because the dialogue is about the specific requirements for a particular course, not a general rule about professors teaching courses.
reason.id: Opsi [2] salah karena dialog tersebut tentang persyaratan spesifik untuk mata kuliah tertentu, bukan aturan umum tentang profesor yang mengajar mata kuliah.

options.text: The professor required the class to prepare an outline.

reason.en: Option [3] is incorrect because it reverses the meaning. The professor outlined the requirements, meaning she explained what the students needed to do, not that the students were required to prepare an outline.
reason.id: Opsi [3] salah karena membalikkan makna. Profesor menguraikan persyaratan, yang berarti dia menjelaskan apa yang perlu dilakukan siswa, bukan bahwa siswa diharuskan menyiapkan garis besar.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) How did Chuck look when you visited him in the hospital?
(man)He's looked better.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Chuck had improved.

reason.en: This is incorrect because 'He's looked better' is an understatement. It implies that he did not look good, rather than that he had improved.
reason.id: Ini salah karena 'He's looked better' adalah pernyataan yang meremehkan. Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak terlihat baik, daripada dia telah membaik.

options.text: This visit was better than the last.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue is about Chuck's appearance in the hospital, not a comparison between visits.
reason.id: Ini salah. Percakapan tersebut adalah tentang penampilan Chuck di rumah sakit, bukan perbandingan antara kunjungan.

options.text: Chuck looked at him in the hospital.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man is describing Chuck's appearance, not what Chuck was looking at.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pria itu sedang menggambarkan penampilan Chuck, bukan apa yang dilihat Chuck.

options.text: Chuck didn't seem to be doing very well.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the phrase 'He's looked better' is an idiomatic expression used to mean that someone does not look well. It's an understatement, implying the opposite of what is literally said. Therefore, Chuck didn't seem to be doing very well.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, frasa 'He's looked better' adalah ungkapan idiomatik yang digunakan untuk menyatakan bahwa seseorang tidak terlihat baik. Ini adalah pernyataan yang meremehkan, menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang dikatakan secara harfiah. Oleh karena itu, Chuck tampaknya tidak dalam kondisi yang baik.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)How much was tuition increased for next month?
(woman) More than I can afford.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She thinks the tuition should be raised.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'More than I can afford' directly contradicts the idea that she thinks tuition should be raised. This is an incorrect interpretation of her financial situation.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'More than I can afford' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia berpikir biaya kuliah harus dinaikkan. Ini adalah interpretasi yang salah tentang situasi keuangannya.

options.text: The semesters tuition is quite affordable.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'More than I can afford' means the opposite of affordable. This option misinterprets the meaning of the idiom.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'More than I can afford' berarti kebalikan dari terjangkau. Opsi ini salah menafsirkan arti ungkapan tersebut.

options.text: She doesn't have enough money for her school fees.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the phrase 'More than I can afford' is a common idiom that means the speaker does not have enough money to pay for something. In this context, it refers to her school fees (tuition).
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, frasa 'More than I can afford' adalah idiom umum yang berarti pembicara tidak punya cukup uang untuk membayar sesuatu. Dalam konteks ini, ini merujuk pada biaya sekolahnya (tuition).

options.text: She has more than enough for tuition.

reason.en: The phrase 'More than I can afford' explicitly states that she cannot afford the cost, making this option the direct opposite of what she means.
reason.id: Frasa 'More than I can afford' secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia tidak mampu membayar biayanya, menjadikan opsi ini kebalikan langsung dari apa yang dia maksud.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) How were the grades on the history exam.
(man)No one got above a C.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks he got a good grade.

reason.en: The man's statement 'No one got above a C' implies that the grades were low, not that he thought he got a good grade. This answer misinterprets the negative implication.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'Tidak ada yang mendapat nilai di atas C' menyiratkan bahwa nilainya rendah, bukan bahwa dia berpikir dia mendapat nilai bagus. Jawaban ini salah menafsirkan implikasi negatif.

options.text: The history grades were all C or above.

reason.en: This answer directly contradicts the man's statement. He said 'No one got above a C,' which means the highest grade was a C, not that all grades were C or above.
reason.id: Jawaban ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria tersebut. Dia berkata 'Tidak ada yang mendapat nilai di atas C,' yang berarti nilai tertinggi adalah C, bukan bahwa semua nilai adalah C atau di atasnya.

options.text: No one got history grades.

reason.en: This answer is a misinterpretation. The dialogue is about the grades received on the exam, not about whether grades were issued.
reason.id: Jawaban ini adalah salah tafsir. Dialog tersebut membahas nilai yang diterima pada ujian, bukan tentang apakah nilai dikeluarkan.

options.text: There were no high scores.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, the phrase 'No one got above a C' is an indirect way of saying that the highest grade achieved was a C, meaning there were no scores higher than a C. This is a correct interpretation of the negative statement.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, frasa 'Tidak ada yang mendapat nilai di atas C' adalah cara tidak langsung untuk mengatakan bahwa nilai tertinggi yang dicapai adalah C, yang berarti tidak ada skor yang lebih tinggi dari C. Ini adalah interpretasi yang benar dari pernyataan negatif tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
(woman) You know, this is the second time this week that you've been late to class!
(man)It was impossible to find a place to park before the ten o'clock class!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The parking lots were full before 10:00.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, the man's statement 'It was impossible to find a place to park before the ten o'clock class!' implies that the parking lots were full. The context of being late to class due to parking issues directly supports this conclusion.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, pernyataan pria 'It was impossible to find a place to park before the ten o'clock class!' menyiratkan bahwa tempat parkir penuh. Konteks terlambat masuk kelas karena masalah parkir secara langsung mendukung kesimpulan ini.

options.text: It was impossible to start class by 10:00.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the man's statement. He is talking about the difficulty of finding parking, not the possibility of starting the class itself.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan pernyataan pria tersebut. Dia berbicara tentang kesulitan menemukan tempat parkir, bukan tentang kemungkinan memulai kelas itu sendiri.

options.text: He parked the car before class at 10:00.

reason.en: This option contradicts the man's statement. He said it was impossible to find a place to park, implying he did not park before class.
reason.id: Opsi ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria tersebut. Dia mengatakan tidak mungkin menemukan tempat parkir, yang menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak parkir sebelum kelas.

options.text: The possibility of finding a place to park increased.

reason.en: This option is the opposite of what the man implied. His statement indicates that the possibility of finding parking decreased or was non-existent.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat oleh pria tersebut. Pernyataannya menunjukkan bahwa kemungkinan menemukan tempat parkir berkurang atau tidak ada sama sekali.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
(man)Can I help you find something?
(woman) Yes, thank you. I need to get a new rug.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's found a new ring.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because the word 'rug' sounds similar to 'ring', which is a common trap in Part A of the Listening section. The dialogue is about home furnishings, not jewelry.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena kata 'rug' terdengar mirip dengan 'ring', yang merupakan jebakan umum di Bagian A dari bagian Mendengarkan. Percakapan ini tentang perabotan rumah tangga, bukan perhiasan.

options.text: She needs to help him find something.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misinterprets the roles in the dialogue. The man offers help, and the woman accepts it, stating her need. This option reverses that dynamic.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah menafsirkan peran dalam dialog. Pria itu menawarkan bantuan, dan wanita itu menerimanya, menyatakan kebutuhannya. Opsi ini membalikkan dinamika tersebut.

options.text: She's shopping for a carpet.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the correct answer uses a synonym for 'rug'. 'Carpet' is a synonym for 'rug', and the woman explicitly states she needs a new one.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, jawaban yang benar menggunakan sinonim untuk 'rug'. 'Carpet' adalah sinonim untuk 'rug', dan wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia membutuhkan yang baru.

options.text: She's thankful she has a rag.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it mishears 'rug' as 'rag'. While they sound somewhat similar, 'rag' refers to a piece of old cloth, which is not what the woman is looking for. She is shopping for a floor covering.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah mendengar 'rug' sebagai 'rag'. Meskipun terdengar agak mirip, 'rag' mengacu pada sepotong kain bekas, yang bukan yang dicari wanita itu. Dia sedang berbelanja untuk penutup lantai.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) I'd like to open an account.
(man)Would you like a savings account or an interest-bearing checking account?
questions.question: 9. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSA TION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: In a department store.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue is about opening an account, which is a banking service, not typically offered in a department store.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tersebut tentang membuka rekening, yang merupakan layanan perbankan, dan biasanya tidak ditawarkan di department store.

options.text: In a bank.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 21: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where, the conversation likely takes place in a bank. The phrases 'open an account,' 'savings account,' and 'interest-bearing checking account' are all terms directly related to banking services.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 21: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where, percakapan ini kemungkinan besar terjadi di bank. Frasa 'membuka rekening', 'rekening tabungan', dan 'rekening giro berbunga' adalah istilah-istilah yang secara langsung berkaitan dengan layanan perbankan.

options.text: In an accounting firm.

reason.en: This is incorrect. An accounting firm deals with financial records and taxes, but the specific services mentioned (opening savings or checking accounts) are characteristic of a bank.
reason.id: Ini salah. Firma akuntansi menangani catatan keuangan dan pajak, tetapi layanan spesifik yang disebutkan (membuka rekening tabungan atau giro) adalah ciri khas bank.

options.text: In a checkout line.

reason.en: This is incorrect. While a checkout line involves a transaction, it is for purchasing goods, not for opening financial accounts.
reason.id: Ini salah. Meskipun antrean kasir melibatkan transaksi, itu adalah untuk membeli barang, bukan untuk membuka rekening keuangan.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART B: LONG CONVERSATIONS (Skills 18–22) - SKILL 21: DRAW CONCLUSIONS ABOUT WHO, WHAT, WHEN, WHERE

questions.passage:
(woman) Why does Jane spend so much time in San Francisco?
(man)She has a cousin there, so she likes to visit, especially during the holidays.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Jane usually visits San Francisco for her vacations.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man implies that Jane visits San Francisco during holidays because she likes to visit her cousin. The phrase 'especially during the holidays' suggests that holidays are a common or preferred time for these visits, which aligns with the idea of vacation visits.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pria itu menyiratkan bahwa Jane mengunjungi San Francisco selama liburan karena dia suka mengunjungi sepupunya. Frasa 'terutama selama liburan' menunjukkan bahwa liburan adalah waktu yang umum atau disukai untuk kunjungan ini, yang selaras dengan gagasan kunjungan liburan.

options.text: Jane's cousin often visits San Francisco.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue states Jane visits her cousin, not that the cousin visits San Francisco often.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog menyatakan Jane mengunjungi sepupunya, bukan bahwa sepupu itu sering mengunjungi San Francisco.

options.text: Whenever there's a holiday, Jane's cousin goes to San Francisco.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue states Jane visits her cousin, not that the cousin goes to San Francisco for holidays.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog menyatakan Jane mengunjungi sepupunya, bukan bahwa sepupu itu pergi ke San Francisco untuk liburan.

options.text: Whenever there's a holiday, Jane leaves San Francisco.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue states Jane visits San Francisco during holidays, not that she leaves San Francisco.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog menyatakan Jane mengunjungi San Francisco selama liburan, bukan bahwa dia meninggalkan San Francisco.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you really hungry?
(man)I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks.
questions.question: 11.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'd really like to have something to eat.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks' is an exaggeration to express that he is very hungry and would like to eat. Option [0] accurately paraphrases this sentiment.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks' adalah sebuah hiperbola untuk mengungkapkan bahwa dia sangat lapar dan ingin makan. Pilihan [0] memparafrasekan sentimen ini secara akurat.

options.text: Because he's weak, he can't eat.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the man's statement. He is expressing extreme hunger, not physical weakness preventing him from eating.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan pernyataan pria tersebut. Dia mengungkapkan rasa lapar yang ekstrem, bukan kelemahan fisik yang mencegahnya makan.

options.text: It's been weeks since he's had anything to eat.

reason.en: This option takes the man's hyperbolic statement literally. While it's related to the time frame, it doesn't capture the intended meaning of extreme hunger as well as option [0]. The phrase 'haven't eaten in weeks' is an idiom for being very hungry, not a literal statement of fact.
reason.id: Opsi ini menganggap pernyataan hiperbolik pria itu secara harfiah. Meskipun terkait dengan kerangka waktu, opsi ini tidak menangkap makna yang dimaksudkan yaitu rasa lapar yang ekstrem sebaik opsi [0]. Frasa 'haven't eaten in weeks' adalah idiom untuk sangat lapar, bukan pernyataan fakta harfiah.

options.text: He hasn't felt like eating for weeks.

reason.en: This option reverses the meaning. The man is expressing a strong desire to eat due to hunger, not a lack of desire to eat over several weeks.
reason.id: Opsi ini membalikkan makna. Pria itu mengungkapkan keinginan kuat untuk makan karena lapar, bukan kurangnya keinginan untuk makan selama beberapa minggu.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)The traffic outside is really loud!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Traffic should not be allowed.

reason.en: The woman's response 'I'll say!' is an expression of agreement, not a statement about traffic regulations.
reason.id: Respons wanita 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, bukan pernyataan tentang peraturan lalu lintas.

options.text: She thinks that the traffic should stay Outside.

reason.en: The phrase 'I'll say!' indicates agreement with the previous statement about the loudness of the traffic, not a desire for the traffic to remain outside.
reason.id: Frasa 'I'll say!' menunjukkan persetujuan dengan pernyataan sebelumnya tentang kerasnya suara lalu lintas, bukan keinginan agar lalu lintas tetap di luar.

options.text: She agrees that the traffic is noisy.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the woman's response 'I'll say!' is a common idiom used to strongly agree with the previous speaker. The man commented on the loud traffic, and the woman is agreeing that it is indeed noisy.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, respons wanita 'I'll say!' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan pembicara sebelumnya. Pria itu berkomentar tentang lalu lintas yang bising, dan wanita itu setuju bahwa itu memang berisik.

options.text: She'll stay outside with the man.

reason.en: The woman's agreement is about the noise level of the traffic, not about her intention to stay outside with the man.
reason.id: Persetujuan wanita itu adalah tentang tingkat kebisingan lalu lintas, bukan tentang niatnya untuk tetap berada di luar bersama pria itu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 11 - Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) Have you seen the headlines yet
today?
I haven't had a chance to read a word.
questions.question: 13.WHAT ARE THEY PROBABLY DIS CUSSING?

options.text: The headings for today's reading assignment.

reason.en: The woman asks about 'headlines' and the man hasn't read 'a word,' implying he hasn't seen the news. The context is not about a reading assignment.
reason.id: Wanita bertanya tentang 'headlines' dan pria itu belum membaca 'sepotong kata pun', menyiratkan bahwa dia belum melihat berita. Konteksnya bukan tentang tugas membaca.

options.text: The chance to make the headlines.

reason.en: While 'headlines' can relate to achieving fame, the phrase 'read a word' strongly suggests a written source of news, not personal achievement.
reason.id: Meskipun 'headlines' dapat berhubungan dengan pencapaian ketenaran, frasa 'read a word' sangat menyarankan sumber berita tertulis, bukan pencapaian pribadi.

options.text: Her reading ability.

reason.en: The conversation is about 'headlines' and reading them, not about the man's reading ability.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang 'headlines' dan membacanya, bukan tentang kemampuan membaca pria itu.

options.text: The daily newspaper.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, the phrase 'headlines' in the context of 'today' and 'read a word' strongly implies a newspaper or news source. The man's response indicates he hasn't had the opportunity to read the news.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, frasa 'headlines' dalam konteks 'hari ini' dan 'membaca sepatah kata pun' sangat menyiratkan surat kabar atau sumber berita. Respons pria itu menunjukkan bahwa dia belum sempat membaca berita.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 4 - Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
(man)I'm not ready yet, and it's going to take me a while longer.
(woman) You'd better hurry. Take five minutes too long, and you'll miss the bus.
questions.question: 14. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The bus trip is only five minutes long.

(narrator)    I saw a television program about it last night, and according to the program, there's about 3,000 tons of metal out there in space, traveling at speeds around 17,000 miles per hour.

(woman)       Well, it comes from all those space missions that have gone up since 1957. Every time a rocket ship goes up into space, it leaves a lot behind, and this stuff goes into orbit around the Earth: booster rockets, solar panels, remnants of satellites, and even nuclear reactors.
(man)         Isn't it dangerous to have all this stuff out there?
(woman)       Some space scientists are worried about possible collisions between this orbiting junk and spaceships, particu larly manned spacecrafts; however, so far there haven't been any such acci dents.
(man)         Well, I hope that they're going to do something about this, both for the sake of safety and for the sake of the environment.
(woman)       Me, too. I know that right now the problem is being studied by numer ous scientists; hopefully, they'll be able to find solutions before the prob lem gets too much worse.

parts.title: Part C
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear several talks. After each talk, you will hear some 
questions. The talks and questions will not be repeated. 
After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best 
answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 
Here is an example. 
On the recording, you hear: 
(narrator) Listen to an instructor talk to his class about painting. 
(man) Artist Grant Wood was a guiding force in the school'of painting known as American 
regionalist, a style reflecting the distinctive characteristics of art from rural areas of 
the United States. Wood began drawing animals on the family farm at the age of three, 
and when he was thirty-eight one of his paintings received a remarkable amount of 
. public notice and acclaim. This painting, called "American Gothic," is a starkly 
simple depiction of a serious couple staring directly out at the viewer. 

Now listen to a sample question.
(narrator) What style of painting is known as American regionalist?

In your test book, you read:
(A) Art from America s inner cities
(B) Art from the central region of the United States 
(C) Art from various urban areas in the United States 
(D) Art from rural sections of America 

The best answer to the question "What style of painting is known as American regionalist?" is (D), "Art from rural sections of America." Therefore, the correct choice is (D). 

Now listen to another sample question.
(narrator) What is the name of Wood's most successful painting? 
In your test book, you read:
(A) "American Regionalist"
(B) "The Family Farm in Iowa"
(C) "American Gothic" 
(D) "A Serious Couple" 

The best answer to the question, "What is the name of Wood's most successful painting?" is (C), "American Gothic." Therefore, the correct choice is (C). 

Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

questions.question: 39.  WHEN DOES THIS TALK PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: On the first day of class

reason.en: The professor mentions that the paper is due in two weeks and outlines are due by Friday of the same week. This indicates that the semester is already in progress, not at the very beginning.
reason.id: Profesor menyebutkan bahwa tugas makalah akan dikumpulkan dalam dua minggu dan kerangka tugas dikumpulkan pada hari Jumat minggu yang sama. Hal ini menunjukkan bahwa semester sudah berjalan, bukan di awal.

options.text: In the middle of the semester

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 25: Infer Who, What, When, Where, the professor states the paper is due in two weeks and outlines are due by Friday. This timing, with a significant assignment deadline approaching, is typical of the middle of a semester.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 25: Infer Who, What, When, Where, profesor menyatakan bahwa makalah akan dikumpulkan dalam dua minggu dan kerangka tugas dikumpulkan pada hari Jumat. Waktu ini, dengan tenggat waktu tugas yang signifikan, khas untuk pertengahan semester.

options.text: At the end of class

reason.en: The talk is about an upcoming assignment (papers due in two weeks, outlines due Friday) and involves research. This suggests it's not just a brief announcement at the very end of class, but a more substantial discussion about a major assignment.
reason.id: Pembicaraan ini adalah tentang tugas yang akan datang (makalah dikumpulkan dalam dua minggu, kerangka dikumpulkan pada hari Jumat) dan melibatkan penelitian. Ini menunjukkan bahwa ini bukan sekadar pengumuman singkat di akhir kelas, tetapi diskusi yang lebih substansial tentang tugas utama.

options.text: In the final week of the semester

reason.en: The professor is discussing an assignment that is due in two weeks. If it were the final week of the semester, the assignment would likely be due much sooner or already completed, not with a two-week deadline.
reason.id: Profesor sedang membahas tugas yang akan dikumpulkan dalam dua minggu. Jika ini adalah minggu terakhir semester, tugas tersebut kemungkinan besar akan dikumpulkan lebih cepat atau sudah selesai, bukan dengan tenggat waktu dua minggu.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - PART C — LONG TALKS / LECTURES - Skill 25: Infer Who, What, When, Where

questions.question: 40.  WHEN IS THE PAPER DUE?

options.text: Later today

reason.en: This is incorrect because the professor states the paper is due in two weeks, not later today.
reason.id: Ini salah karena profesor menyatakan bahwa tugas tersebut dikumpulkan dalam dua minggu, bukan hari ini.

options.text: By Friday of this week

reason.en: This is incorrect. The professor mentions Friday of this week as the deadline for the outlines, not the final paper.
reason.id: Ini salah. Profesor menyebutkan Jumat minggu ini sebagai tenggat waktu untuk kerangka, bukan untuk tugas akhir.

options.text: In two weeks

reason.en: Based on Listening Part C - Skill 25: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where, the professor explicitly states, 'The paper itself is due in two weeks,' making this the correct answer.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Part C - Skill 25: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where, profesor secara eksplisit menyatakan, 'The paper itself is due in two weeks,' menjadikan ini jawaban yang benar.

options.text: In three weeks

reason.en: This is incorrect. The professor specifies a two-week deadline for the paper, not three weeks.
reason.id: Ini salah. Profesor menetapkan tenggat waktu dua minggu untuk tugas tersebut, bukan tiga minggu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part C - Long Talks/Lectures - Skill 25: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where

questions.question: 41.  WHAT TYPES OF REFERENCES SHOULD BE USED IN WRITING THE PAPER?

options.text: Journal and magazine articles

reason.en: This is incorrect because the professor specifically mentions books and journal articles, but does not mention magazine articles as required sources.
reason.id: Ini salah karena profesor secara spesifik menyebutkan buku dan artikel jurnal, tetapi tidak menyebutkan artikel majalah sebagai sumber yang dibutuhkan.

options.text: Books from outside the library

reason.en: This is incorrect because the professor states that students should be spending time in the library for research, implying that the books should be from the library.
reason.id: Ini salah karena profesor menyatakan bahwa siswa harus menghabiskan waktu di perpustakaan untuk riset, menyiratkan bahwa buku-buku tersebut harus berasal dari perpustakaan.

options.text: Books listed in student journals

reason.en: This is incorrect because the professor mentions books and journal articles, not student journals. The phrase 'student journals' is not mentioned in the lecture.
reason.id: Ini salah karena profesor menyebutkan buku dan artikel jurnal, bukan jurnal siswa. Frasa 'jurnal siswa' tidak disebutkan dalam kuliah.

options.text: Both books and journals

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk, the professor explicitly states that students 'need to do some research for this paper, so you should be spending some time in the library. I would like you to have at least three books and at least three recent journal articles as sources.' This directly supports the use of both books and journals.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 23: Antisipasi Topik Sebuah Pembicaraan, profesor secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa siswa 'perlu melakukan riset untuk makalah ini, jadi Anda harus menghabiskan waktu di perpustakaan. Saya ingin Anda memiliki setidaknya tiga buku dan setidaknya tiga artikel jurnal terbaru sebagai sumber.' Ini secara langsung mendukung penggunaan buku dan jurnal.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING Part C — Long Talks / Lectures (Skills 23–27)

questions.question: 42.  HOW MANY TOTAL PAGES SHOULD BE IN THE PAPER, INCLUDING THE TITLE PAGE AND THE REFERENCE LIST?

options.text: Two

reason.en: This is incorrect. The professor states the paper should be five pages, plus a title page and a reference list, totaling more than two pages.
reason.id: Ini salah. Profesor menyatakan bahwa makalah tersebut harus terdiri dari lima halaman, ditambah halaman judul dan daftar referensi, dengan total lebih dari dua halaman.

options.text: Three

reason.en: This is incorrect. The professor specifies five pages of composition, plus a title page and a reference list, making the total more than three pages.
reason.id: Ini salah. Profesor menentukan lima halaman komposisi, ditambah halaman judul dan daftar referensi, sehingga totalnya lebih dari tiga halaman.

options.text: Five

reason.en: This is incorrect. While the composition itself is five pages, the question asks for the total number of pages including the title page and reference list.
reason.id: Ini salah. Meskipun komposisinya sendiri lima halaman, pertanyaan tersebut menanyakan jumlah total halaman termasuk halaman judul dan daftar referensi.

options.text: Seven

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 25: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where, the professor explicitly states the paper should be 'five pages long; in addition to the five pages of composition, you should have a tide page and a one-page reference list'. This means 5 (composition) + 1 (title page) + 1 (reference list) = 7 pages.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 25: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where, profesor secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa makalah tersebut harus 'lima halaman; selain lima halaman komposisi, Anda harus memiliki halaman judul dan daftar referensi satu halaman'. Ini berarti 5 (komposisi) + 1 (halaman judul) + 1 (daftar referensi) = 7 halaman.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - PART C: LONG TALKS / LECTURES - Skill 25: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, When, Where

questions.question: 43.  IN WHAT SEASON OF THE YEAR WILL THE TRIP TAKE PLACE?

options.text: In winter

reason.en: The speaker mentions that the temperature is an average for springtime and that it only gets a few degrees warmer in the summer and cooler in the winter, but the trip is specifically stated to be in the springtime.
reason.id: Pembicara menyebutkan bahwa suhu adalah rata-rata untuk musim semi dan bahwa suhu hanya menjadi beberapa derajat lebih hangat di musim panas dan lebih dingin di musim dingin, tetapi perjalanan secara spesifik dinyatakan akan dilakukan di musim semi.

options.text: In spring

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk, the speaker explicitly states, 'This is the average daily temperature in the springtime, when we will be there'. This directly answers the question about the season of the trip.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk, pembicara secara eksplisit menyatakan, 'This is the average daily temperature in the springtime, when we will be there' (Ini adalah suhu harian rata-rata di musim semi, saat kita akan berada di sana). Ini secara langsung menjawab pertanyaan tentang musim perjalanan.

options.text: In summer

reason.en: The speaker mentions that the temperature only gets a few degrees warmer in the summer, implying that summer is not the season of the trip, which is stated to be in the spring.
reason.id: Pembicara menyebutkan bahwa suhu hanya menjadi beberapa derajat lebih hangat di musim panas, menyiratkan bahwa musim panas bukanlah musim perjalanan, yang dinyatakan akan dilakukan di musim semi.

options.text: In fall

reason.en: The text does not mention fall at all. The speaker focuses on spring, summer, and winter temperatures in relation to the spring trip.
reason.id: Teks sama sekali tidak menyebutkan musim gugur. Pembicara berfokus pada suhu musim semi, musim panas, dan musim dingin sehubungan dengan perjalanan musim semi.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Part C: Long Talks / Lectures (Skills 23–27) - Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk

questions.question: 44.  WHAT IS THE WEATHER LIKE IN HAWAII?

options.text: Seasonable, with warm summers and cold winters

reason.en: This is incorrect because the passage explicitly states that Hawaii's temperature only gets 'a few degrees warmer in the summer and a few degrees cooler in the winter,' contradicting the idea of 'warm summers and cold winters.'
reason.id: Ini salah karena bagian tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa suhu Hawaii hanya 'sedikit lebih hangat di musim panas dan sedikit lebih dingin di musim dingin,' yang bertentangan dengan gagasan 'musim panas yang hangat dan musim dingin yang dingin.'

options.text: Fairly constant and moderate

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk, the passage describes the weather in Hawaii as 'constant and moderate' due to the trade winds, which is directly supported by the text stating the average daily temperature is around 80 degrees Fahrenheit year-round and the trade winds keep humidity down.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 23: Antisipasi Topik Sebuah Pembicaraan, bagian tersebut menggambarkan cuaca di Hawaii sebagai 'konstan dan sedang' karena angin pasat, yang secara langsung didukung oleh teks yang menyatakan bahwa suhu rata-rata harian adalah sekitar 80 derajat Fahrenheit sepanjang tahun dan angin pasat menjaga kelembaban tetap rendah.

options.text: Very humid

reason.en: This is incorrect because the passage states that the trade winds 'also keep the humidity down, which makes the weather even more pleasant,' directly contradicting the idea that the weather is 'very humid.'
reason.id: Ini salah karena bagian tersebut menyatakan bahwa angin pasat 'juga menjaga kelembaban tetap rendah, yang membuat cuaca semakin menyenangkan,' yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa cuaca 'sangat lembab.'

options.text: Extremely hot year-round

reason.en: This is incorrect because the passage clearly states the average daily temperature is 'about 80 degrees Fahrenheit' and mentions it only gets 'a few degrees warmer in the summer and a few degrees cooler in the winter,' which is not 'extremely hot year-round.'
reason.id: Ini salah karena bagian tersebut dengan jelas menyatakan suhu rata-rata harian adalah 'sekitar 80 derajat Fahrenheit' dan menyebutkan bahwa suhu hanya 'sedikit lebih hangat di musim panas dan sedikit lebih dingin di musim dingin,' yang bukanlah 'sangat panas sepanjang tahun.'

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part C: Long Talks / Lectures (Skills 23–27)

questions.question: 45.  WHAT IS TRUE ABOUT THE TRADE WINDS?

options.text: They come from the Southwest.

reason.en: The passage states that the trade winds blow from the northeast, not the southwest.
reason.id: Teks menyatakan bahwa angin pasat bertiup dari timur laut, bukan barat daya.

options.text: They come most days of the year.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 25 (During Listening - Infer Who/What/Where), the passage explicitly states that the trade winds 'blow through the islands an average of slightly more than 300 days per year,' which means they come most days of the year.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 25 (During Listening - Infer Who/What/Where), teks secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa angin pasat 'bertiup melalui pulau-pulau rata-rata lebih dari 300 hari per tahun,' yang berarti angin tersebut datang hampir setiap hari dalam setahun.

options.text: They are the hardest during the night.

reason.en: The passage mentions that the trade winds 'are the strongest during the heat of the afternoon and turn into a cooling breeze in the evening,' not that they are hardest at night.
reason.id: Teks menyebutkan bahwa angin pasat 'paling kuat selama panasnya sore hari dan berubah menjadi angin sepoi-sepoi yang menyejukkan di malam hari,' bukan bahwa angin tersebut paling kuat di malam hari.

options.text: They increase the humidity.

reason.en: The passage states that the trade winds 'also keep the humidity down,' which is the opposite of increasing humidity.
reason.id: Teks menyatakan bahwa angin pasat 'juga menjaga kelembaban tetap rendah,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari meningkatkan kelembaban.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING, Part C — Long Talks / Lectures (Skills 23–27), Skill 25: During Listening - Infer Who/What/Where

questions.question:
WHAT WILL THE PEOPLE LISTENING TO THE 
TALK PROBABLY BE DOING SOON?

options.text: Preparing for a trip

reason.en: The speaker is providing information specifically for people who are taking a trip to Hawaii next week, discussing expected weather conditions and clothing choices. This directly implies they are preparing for the trip. This aligns with the overall purpose of the talk, which is to inform trip participants.
reason.id: Pembicara memberikan informasi khusus untuk orang-orang yang akan melakukan perjalanan ke Hawaii minggu depan, membahas kondisi cuaca yang diharapkan dan pilihan pakaian. Hal ini secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa mereka sedang bersiap untuk perjalanan tersebut. Ini sejalan dengan tujuan keseluruhan dari pembicaraan, yaitu untuk menginformasikan peserta perjalanan.

options.text: Writing a report about the weather

reason.en: The talk is about weather in Hawaii for an upcoming trip, not a formal report. The speaker is giving advice, not assigning a task to write a report. The context is informal and preparatory.
reason.id: Pembicaraan ini tentang cuaca di Hawaii untuk perjalanan yang akan datang, bukan laporan formal. Pembicara memberikan saran, bukan menugaskan tugas untuk menulis laporan. Konteksnya informal dan persiapan.

options.text: Beginning a study of the weather

reason.en: The talk is not introducing a new subject of study but is providing specific, practical information for an imminent event (the trip). The listeners are not beginning a general study of weather; they are receiving targeted information for a specific purpose.
reason.id: Pembicaraan ini tidak memperkenalkan subjek studi baru tetapi memberikan informasi spesifik dan praktis untuk acara yang akan segera terjadi (perjalanan). Pendengar tidak memulai studi umum tentang cuaca; mereka menerima informasi yang ditargetkan untuk tujuan tertentu.

options.text: Buying warm clothes for a trip

reason.en: While clothing is mentioned, the talk emphasizes the moderate temperature and cooling breezes, suggesting that extreme 'warm' clothing might not be necessary. The primary focus is on general weather information for the trip, not specifically on buying warm clothes.
reason.id: Meskipun pakaian disebutkan, pembicaraan ini menekankan suhu sedang dan angin sepoi-sepoi yang sejuk, menunjukkan bahwa pakaian 'hangat' yang ekstrem mungkin tidak diperlukan. Fokus utamanya adalah pada informasi cuaca umum untuk perjalanan, bukan secara khusus pada pembelian pakaian hangat.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part C - Long Talks / Lectures (Skills 23-27) - Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk

questions.question: 47.  THIS LECTURE WOULD PROBABLY BE A PART OF WHICH COURSE?

options.text: Modern American Authors

reason.en: While Walt Whitman is an American author, the focus on his poetry collection 'Leaves of Grass' and specific poems like 'Song of Myself' and 'When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed' points more specifically to a literature course rather than a general course on modern American authors.
reason.id: Meskipun Walt Whitman adalah penulis Amerika, fokus pada kumpulan puisinya 'Leaves of Grass' dan puisi spesifik seperti 'Song of Myself' dan 'When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed' lebih mengarah pada mata kuliah sastra daripada mata kuliah umum tentang penulis Amerika modern.

options.text: United States History

reason.en: Although Abraham Lincoln's death is mentioned as a context for one of Whitman's poems, the lecture's primary focus is on Whitman's literary work, not on historical events or the broader sweep of United States History.
reason.id: Meskipun kematian Abraham Lincoln disebutkan sebagai konteks untuk salah satu puisi Whitman, fokus utama kuliah ini adalah pada karya sastra Whitman, bukan pada peristiwa sejarah atau gambaran umum Sejarah Amerika Serikat.

options.text: American Democracy

reason.en: While 'Leaves of Grass' is described as a 'celebration of America, full of pride in the United States and reverence for the goals of American democracy,' this is presented as a theme within his poetry, not the sole or primary focus of a course on American Democracy itself.
reason.id: Meskipun 'Leaves of Grass' digambarkan sebagai 'perayaan Amerika, penuh kebanggaan pada Amerika Serikat dan penghormatan terhadap tujuan demokrasi Amerika,' ini disajikan sebagai tema dalam puisinya, bukan fokus tunggal atau utama dari mata kuliah tentang Demokrasi Amerika itu sendiri.

options.text: Nineteenth-Century American Literature

reason.en: Based on Reading Skill 13: Determine the Tone, Purpose, or Course, the lecture discusses Walt Whitman, an 'American poet and author of the renowned collection of poems Leaves of Grass,' and analyzes specific poems and their themes. This content is most appropriate for a course on Nineteenth-Century American Literature.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Reading Skill 13: Determine the Tone, Purpose, or Course, kuliah ini membahas Walt Whitman, seorang 'penyair dan penulis Amerika dari kumpulan puisi terkenal Leaves of Grass,' dan menganalisis puisi spesifik serta tema-temanya. Konten ini paling sesuai untuk mata kuliah Sastra Amerika Abad Kesembilan Belas.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION THREE — READING COMPREHENSION, Skill 13 — Determine the Tone, Purpose, or Course

questions.question: 48.  WHAT IS THE MOST COMMON THEME IN LEAVES OF GRASS?

options.text: The death of Abraham Lincoln

reason.en: This is too specific. While the death of Abraham Lincoln is mentioned, it is only one event discussed in relation to the collection, not the overarching theme.
reason.id: Ini terlalu spesifik. Meskipun kematian Abraham Lincoln disebutkan, itu hanyalah satu peristiwa yang dibahas sehubungan dengan kumpulan puisi tersebut, bukan tema utamanya.

options.text: The beauty of American democracy

reason.en: Based on Reading Skill 1: Answer Main Idea Questions Correctly, the passage explicitly states that 'Leaves of Grass' is 'a celebration of America, full of pride in the United States and reverence for the goals of American democracy.' This is presented as the primary theme.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Reading Skill 1: Answer Main Idea Questions Correctly, bagian bacaan secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa 'Leaves of Grass' adalah 'perayaan Amerika, penuh kebanggaan pada Amerika Serikat dan penghormatan terhadap tujuan demokrasi Amerika.' Ini disajikan sebagai tema utama.

options.text: The raising of plants

reason.en: This is irrelevant to the content discussed. The passage focuses on poetry and its themes, not horticulture.
reason.id: Ini tidak relevan dengan konten yang dibahas. Bagian bacaan berfokus pada puisi dan temanya, bukan hortikultura.

options.text: The maturity of poetry

reason.en: While the collection grew over time, 'maturity of poetry' is not stated as the most common theme. The passage emphasizes the celebration of America and democracy as the central theme.
reason.id: Meskipun kumpulan puisi tersebut berkembang seiring waktu, 'kedewasaan puisi' tidak dinyatakan sebagai tema yang paling umum. Bagian bacaan menekankan perayaan Amerika dan demokrasi sebagai tema sentral.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION THREE — READING COMPREHENSION, Skill 1 — Answer Main Idea Questions Correctly

questions.question: 49.  WHAT BEST DESCRIBES LEAVES OF GRASS}

options.text: It's a poem about the author.

reason.en: This is incorrect because while Walt Whitman is the author, the collection of poems is not primarily described as being 'about the author' but rather about America and its ideals. The passage states it's a 'celebration of America'.
reason.id: Ini salah karena meskipun Walt Whitman adalah penulisnya, kumpulan puisi tersebut tidak digambarkan terutama sebagai 'tentang penulis' melainkan tentang Amerika dan cita-citanya. Teks menyatakan itu adalah 'perayaan Amerika'.

options.text: It's a poem about Abraham Lincoln.

reason.en: This is incorrect. While the poem 'When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed' was added later and reflects on Abraham Lincoln's death, 'Leaves of Grass' itself is a broader collection and not solely about Lincoln. The passage mentions Lincoln in relation to one specific poem within the collection.
reason.id: Ini salah. Meskipun puisi 'When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed' ditambahkan kemudian dan merefleksikan kematian Abraham Lincoln, 'Leaves of Grass' itu sendiri adalah kumpulan yang lebih luas dan tidak hanya tentang Lincoln. Teks menyebutkan Lincoln terkait dengan satu puisi spesifik dalam kumpulan tersebut.

options.text: It's a collection of twelve poems that remained unchanged.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The passage states that the first edition had only twelve poems, but it also explicitly mentions that 'Several other editions of Leaves of Grass appeared throughout Whitman's lifetime with additional poems; Leaves of Grass grew and matured right along with Whitman.' Therefore, it was not unchanged.
reason.id: Ini salah. Teks menyatakan bahwa edisi pertama hanya berisi dua belas puisi, tetapi juga secara eksplisit menyebutkan bahwa 'Beberapa edisi lain dari Leaves of Grass muncul sepanjang masa hidup Whitman dengan puisi tambahan; Leaves of Grass tumbuh dan matang seiring dengan Whitman.' Oleh karena itu, itu tidak tidak berubah.

options.text: It's a volume of poetry that grew with its author.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk and the information in the passage, this is the best description. The passage states, 'Whitman began writing Leaves of Grass in the middle of the nineteenth century, and the first edition appeared in 1855 with only twelve poems. Several other editions of Leaves of Grass appeared throughout Whitman's lifetime with additional poems; Leaves of Grass grew and matured right along with Whitman.' This directly supports the idea that the volume grew with its author.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 23: Anticipate the Topic of a Talk dan informasi dalam teks, ini adalah deskripsi terbaik. Teks menyatakan, 'Whitman mulai menulis Leaves of Grass pada pertengahan abad kesembilan belas, dan edisi pertama muncul pada tahun 1855 hanya dengan dua belas puisi. Beberapa edisi lain dari Leaves of Grass muncul sepanjang masa hidup Whitman dengan puisi tambahan; Leaves of Grass tumbuh dan matang seiring dengan Whitman.' Ini secara langsung mendukung gagasan bahwa volume tersebut tumbuh bersama penulisnya.

(narrator)    Listen to a talk about Hawaii.
(woman)	For those of you taking part in the trip to Hawaii next week, I'd like to give you a litde information about the weather that you can expect there. You can expect the average daily tem perature there to be about 80 degrees Fahrenheit or 26 degrees Celsius. This is the average daily temperature in the springtime, when we will be there; it is interesting to note that it only gets a few degrees warmer in the summer and a few degrees cooler in the win ter. One important factor that keeps the temperature so constant and moder ate in Hawaii is the trade winds. These are winds that blow in on the north east, or windward, side of the islands on an almost daily basis; the trade winds blow through the islands an average of slighdy more than 300 days per year, and they are the strongest during the heat of the afternoon and turn into a cooling breeze in the evening. The trade winds also keep the humidity down, which makes the weather even more pleasant. I hope this information will help you to understand the weather conditions that you're going to encounter next week on your trip; it should also help you decide what types of clothes you should be packing for your trip.

groups.passage:
Questions 43-46
(narrator) Listen to an instructor talk to her class about Walt Whitman. 
(woman) The topic of today's lecture is Walt Whitman, an American poet and author of the renowned collection of poems Leaves of Grass. This volume of poems is a celebration of America, full of pride in the United States and reverence for the goals of American democracy. 

Whitman began writing Leaves of Grass in the middle of the nineteenth century, and the first edition appeared in 1855 with only twelve poems. Several other editions of Leaves of Grass appeared throughout Whitman's lifetime with additional poems; Leaves of Grass grew and matured right along with Whitman. 

The longest and best-known poem in Leaves of Grass is "Song of Myself," which appeared in the first edition. The poem "When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed" was added to a later edition. This poem was written at the time of Abraham Lincoln's death in 1865 and contained Whitman's reflections on that event. Lincoln's death occurred in April, in the spring, in a season of new life. This poem reflects that spring can be at the same time a period of death and a period of rebirth.
